title: show table regions is wrong for table with global index
description: ## Bug Report Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks! ### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required) a. enable-global-index=true in config file. b. create table and execute show ddl. ```SQL create table p (id int, c int, d int, unique key uidx(c), unique key idx(id)) partition by range (c) ( partition p0 values less than (4), partition p1 values less than (7), partition p2 values less than (10)) ``` ```SQL show table p regions ``` ### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required) ### 3. What did you see instead (Required) ### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), value DECIMAL(10,2), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE partitioned_table (id INTEGER, c INTEGER, d INTEGER, UNIQUE (c), UNIQUE (id));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'Test', 100.50);
INSERT INTO partitioned_table (id, c, d) VALUES (1, 3, 100), (2, 6, 200), (3, 9, 300);
key_bug_features: show table regions for table with global index
